english: mouse-trap, *wa-ldém* (wa ldem) fox-trap, *tsúg-ċe* (tsug che) to put a trap. II. adj. 1. (Schr. ldém-po (Schr. ldem po)) straight, upright; tall, well-made, Mil., prob. also Wdn. — 2 partic. of III., inconstant; unstable, variable, perishable Cs. III. vb., also ldem-ldém-pa (ldem ldem pa) Sch. to move up and down, striking, trembling, vibrating; gśog-sgró ldém-pa (gshog sgro ldem pa) the clapping of wings Mil.; ldem-ldém (ldem ldem) flexible, supple, elastic, pliant.
Ts. = ldebs (ldebs) I., skyai ldér-la (skyai lder la) on the side of a wall, on a wall, e.g. to paint, to scrawl; rii lder (rii lder).

Cs.: '1. toughness, clamminess, 2. potter's clay'. lder-tso (lder tsho) Cs. 1. clay, 2. an idol made of clay Mṅg. — ldér-sku (lder sku) Glr. prob. = ldér-tso (lder tsho) 2.; acc. to others: a picture on a wall. — ldér-bzo (lder bzo) figures modelled of clay, plastic work, ldér-bzoi lha (lder bzoi lha) Zam. = ldér-tso (lder tsho) 2.; lder-bzoi-ldebs (lder bzoi ldebs) Lex. a clay-enclosure (?) — ldér-so (lder so) Glr. 88, 1. 2., by the context also figure, image.

pf. and imp. log (log), vb. n. to zlog-pa (zlog pa), 1. to come back, to return, to go home, to depart. — 2. to come again, often with pyir (phyir), of diseases, = to relapse; in a specific religious sense v. obrás-bu bźi ('bras bu bzhi), frq.; dgrar (dgrar) to come forward again as an enemy, to renew the war (ni f.) Mil. — 3. to change, to undergo a change, as to colour, smell etc. Med.; gyúr-ldog (gyur ldog), and ldog-gyúr (ldog gyur) Mṅg. changeableness, inconstancy, fickleness. — 4. to turn away (vb. n.) las (las) from; blo ldóg-pa (blo ldog pa) id. Thgy.; ṅo ldóg-pa (ngo ldog pa) v. lóg-pa (log pa). The partic. as adj.: dé-las ldóg-pai (de las ldog pai) (the thing) opposed to that, contrary to it, Wdn.; go-ldóg (go ldog) id. Lt.; mgo-ldóg (mgo ldog) Lex.? — Sch. has also ldog-pyé-ba (ldog phye ba) distinguished, different, from each other, and ldóg-pa (ldog pa) reciprocal, mutual, each separately. Cf. lóg-pa (log pa).
1. vb, pf. ldoṅs (ldongs), loṅ (long), to become blind, to be blind; to be infatuated. — 2. adj., also ldóṅs-pa (ldongs pa), mdóṅs-pa (mdongs pa), blind; infatuated. Cf. lóṅ-ba (long ba).
resp. gsol-ldóṅ (gsol ldong), a small churn, used for preparing tea, = gur-gúr (gur gur), v. sub ja (ja). Cf *doṅ-dús* (*dong dus*) Ld. a stave; ldoṅ-rus (ldong rus)?
Cs.: n. of a yellow earth, bole, ochre, used for staining the walls of houses; ldoṅ-ros-sa (ldong ros sa) Lt.
to give or pay back, to return, = klón-pa (klon pa), glón-pa (glon pa), esp. with lan (lan), to answer Dzl.
to apprehend quickly; to be witty, to be quick in repartee Cs.; ldobs-skyén (ldobs skyen) Lex., explained by śés-sla-ba (shes sla ba) understanding readily?
? rag-ldóm-pa (rag ldom pa) is stated to be = rag-lús-pa (rag lus pa) Ld.

less frq. ldúm-bu (ldum bu), often preceded by ro-snyóms (ro snyoms) alms, consisting of food; ldóm-bu byéd-pa (ldom bu byed pa) to ask such alms; ldóm-sa (ldom sa) alms-house, house where beggars receive food; ldóm-bu-ba (ldom bu ba) a person living on alms, a beggar, Mil., Pth.

pf. sdaṅs (sdangs), I. to be angry, wrathful, mi dgá-źiṅ sdáṅ-ste (mi dga' zhing sdang ste) growing angry, flying into a passion Dzl.; gen. c. la (la): to hate, to be inimically disposed, frq.; sdáṅ-bai dgra (sdang bai dgra) opp. to byáms-pai gnyen (byams pai gnyen); sdáṅ-bar séms-pai dgrá-bo (sdang bar sems pai dgra bo) id. Wdn.; kyim-mtses-kyi dgrá-sdaṅ-ba (khyim mtshes kyi dgra sdang ba), or dgrá-bdo-ba (dgra bdo ba) the neighbour's grudge; sdáṅ(-bai) sems (sdang (-bai) sems), sdáṅ-blo (sdang blo), most frq. źe-sdáṅ (zhe sdang), hatred, enmity, hostility, ill-will; (cf. dug (dug)) sdáṅ-ba tams-ċád (sdang ba thams cad) jig-pa ('jig pa) to subdue all hostile powers; sṅar sdáṅ-ba (sngar sdang ba) the former, the old hatred Mil.; sdaṅ-mig (sdang mig) Lex. an angry look, a scowl. II. for gdáṅ-ba (gdang ba).
1. thick (?) gsús-pa sdig (gsus pa sdig) Mṅg. — 2. foundation C., rgyág-pa (rgyag pa) to lay a foundation.
